prediction.change: The glyph after the literal `11` changed: Program A has `/` (div) where Program B has `%` (mod). In the shared sequence `...11/2+...` vs `...11%2+...`, the divide operator `/` was mutated to modulo `%`.
The glyph after `11` changed: `/` (Div) in Program A became `%` (Mod) in Program B — i.e. `11/2` vs `11%2`.
The operator after "11" changed from '/' (div) in A to '%' (mod) in B — the glyph in the "11/2" vs "11%2" position.
The comparison operator in the loop's if-condition: Program A uses `<` (`if i < n`), Program B uses `<=` (`if i <= n`). A '=' was added after the '<'. A prints 10, B prints 15.
In the loop's if-condition comparison operator, Program A uses `<` while Program B uses `<=` (an `=` glyph added after the `<`). This also changes output: A prints 10, B prints 15.
The comparison operator in the if condition changed from '<' (Program A: `if i < n`) to '<=' (Program B: `if i <= n`). A single '=' glyph was inserted after the '<' on the loop's comparison line.
The glyph inside the innermost quote changed from '@' (rot) to '^' (over): position 9 (the character right after '8 3 5[2+['), i.e. [@*] in A became [^*] in B.

The glyph inside the inner quote changed: in Program A it is `@` (rot), in Program B it is `^` (over). Position: the character immediately after `[2+[` (the first glyph of the inner quote body `[...*]`), i.e. `[@*]` in A vs `[^*]` in B.

Inside the inner quote `[...]`, the glyph `@` (rot) in Program A was changed to `^` (over) in Program B. Position: the first character inside the innermost quote at `[2+[_*]...`, i.e. the `@`/`^` in the `[@*]`/`[^*]` subquote (the 11th non-space glyph, immediately after the second `[`).
The subtrahend 'e' in the return expression became 'd': Program A's `return d - e + a * c` versus Program B's `return d - d + a * c` (the token after the first minus sign).
In the return statement, the subtracted operand `e` (Program A: `d - e + a * c`) became `d` (Program B: `d - d + a * c`) — the second operand of the subtraction changed from glyph `e` to `d`.
In the return expression, the subtrahend changed from 'e' to 'd' (Program A: "d - e + a * c" vs Program B: "d - d + a * c"); the single glyph 'e' became 'd'.
The glyph immediately after the inner quote `[5*]` (the 10th character inside the outer `[...]`, i.e. position between `]` and `8`): Program A has `'` (dip) where Program B has `!` (apply).
